name: Self-powered phosphate sensor (SphS/SphR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: A self-powered sensor for phosphate status in water (eutrophication-relevant). The native cyanobacterial SphS/SphR phosphate two-component system gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in photosynthetic Synechocystis.

input.detects: Phosphate availability in water (low Pi triggers the Pho response)
input.operatingRange: SphS/SphR responds across the phosphate-limited transition

sensing.sensorMechanism: Under phosphate limitation SphS phosphorylates SphR, activating Pho-regulon promoters (e.g. PpstS).
sensing.signalFlow: Low Pi -> SphS/SphR activates a Pho promoter -> sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies the reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence reports phosphate status. Self-powered.

parts.name: sgRNA
parts.source: designed against / upstream of the reporter promoter

performance.notes: SphS/SphR phosphate sensing and Synechocystis CRISPRi-a are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Reports phosphate status (inverse to abundance).

safety.notes: Self-powered; suited to surface-water eutrophication surveys.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check Synechocystis 6803 off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform Synechocystis 6803
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a phosphate standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native Synechocystis SphS/SphR phosphate system with CRISPRa
partsSources: Hirani et al. / SphS-SphR phosphate regulation (Synechocystis)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
